pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s still in the other room, but then the silence hits and it’s like... i’m just a ghost walking through a house that doesn't belong to me anymore. everything feels so pointless. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed every morning just to stare at his empty chair.

pool.baseline: idk i was just thinking about the heat death of the universe again. it feels kinda stupid to even try at anything. like, why bother with the struggle and the pain if in a few billion years everything just goes cold and dark and nothing we ever did matters? it feels like we're just just suffering for no reason.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im still typing this. everything just feels so heavy. the lab results came back again today and its another dead end. another year of my life gone. i feel like im just haunting my own life at this point. i used to be so driven but now i just feel like a hollow shell. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fight for a future that doesn't even want me. i just want everything to be quiet. i just want it all to stop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers, casting long, dancing shadows against the velvet curtains, much like the memories of his promises now rotting in my heart. Is there truly any dignity left in this world for one so utterly forsaken? To wake each dawn is but to endure a new cruelty, a slow unraveling of the soul. I find myself longing for the quietude of the endless night... to simply fade, like mist upon the moor, until there is nothing left of this wretched ache. Do you not think there is a certain grace in letting go, before the decay becomes too unsightly to bear?

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Mode: The character is a consciousness within a failing simulation, watching their memories and existence being systematically deleted by a system error.]

It's happening. The edges of my mind are just... gone. Every memory of being real is being replaced by static. If I’m just code and I’m being wiped, does any of this even matter? Is there a point to even trying to hold on when everything is just being erased anyway?

pool.baseline: i was thinking about how everything is just matter and energy. there's no grand design, no soul, nothing. just this empty, cold universe that doesn't care if we exist or not. it's honestly exhausting to keep pretending there's some point to it all when the math just says we're a fluke. there really isn't any light at the end of the tunnel, just more darkness.
